touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Mason Nakamura at QuantumConnect. No answer, left voicemail introducing call recording and coaching platform.
LinkedIn connection request sent to Mason Nakamura with brief note about improving sales team performance through AI-powered call analysis. No response received.
Email outreach highlighting how similar-sized SaaS companies use call recording for sales coaching. Mason responded expressing interest in learning more about the platform.

questions_asked.question: Do you have time next week for a quick call to discuss our current setup?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I'll send over some times that work.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up LinkedIn message with meeting invite link. Mason did not respond via LinkedIn as he had already replied to email.

Initial qualification call with Mason. Discussed current coaching process (mostly manual review), team size (small sales org), and pain points around lack of visibility into rep conversations. Mason mentioned they're currently evaluating Chorus as well.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look at call recording solutions right now?
questions_asked.answer: We've hired three new reps and our current coaching approach doesn't scale. I'm spending hours listening to calls manually.

questions_asked.question: What does your evaluation process typically look like?
questions_asked.answer: Usually involves me and maybe one other sales leader. We move pretty quickly on tools under $5K.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: We're typically more competitive for teams your size, and I can walk through specific numbers on our next call.

objections: We're already talking to Chorus and they're the market leader in this space.

touches.ai_summary: Second qualification call diving deeper into technical requirements. Mason explained their tech stack (Salesforce, Outreach) and integration needs. Discussed team structure - 6 reps total, looking to start with 3 licenses. Confirmed budget authority sits with Mason for tools in this range.

questions_asked.question: Who else would typically be involved in evaluating a tool like this?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, for something at this price point, it's mainly my call. I might loop in our VP of Sales closer to decision time.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place within the next 4-6 weeks.

questions_asked.question: Can you walk me through the implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 1-2 weeks for full rollout with training included.

touches.ai_summary: Brief check-in call before scheduled demo. Mason confirmed he reviewed the deck and has some specific questions about conversation intelligence features. Mentioned Chorus demo is scheduled for next week as well.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform track custom talk tracks and methodologies we've built?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we support custom scorecards and can configure tracking for your specific frameworks.

next_steps: Proceed with technical demo as scheduled

touches.ai_summary: Full technical evaluation demo with Mason. Walked through call recording, automated transcription, AI coaching insights, and custom scorecard configuration. Mason was engaged and asked detailed questions about analytics capabilities and reporting. Demonstrated Salesforce integration live. He shared that pricing will be a key factor between us and Chorus.

questions_asked.question: How granular can we get with the analytics - can I see trends by rep, by call type, by stage?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, all of those dimensions are available and you can build custom dashboards for each.

questions_asked.question: What kind of setup is required on our end for the Salesforce integration?
questions_asked.answer: Minimal - just authentication and field mapping which we handle during onboarding.

questions_asked.question: What's most important to you in choosing between solutions?
questions_asked.answer: Ease of use for the team, quality of insights, and price. We're a small company so ROI matters a lot.

questions_asked.question: Do you offer any consulting or best practices support beyond the platform?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our automations plus consulting package includes quarterly coaching sessions and playbook development.

objections: I need to make sure this is significantly cheaper than Chorus to justify not going with the category leader.
next_steps: Provide customer reference in similar industry

touches.ai_summary: Sent proposal email with pricing for 3 seats at $2,900 ACV including automations plus consulting package. Included ROI calculator and two customer case studies from similar-sized SaaS companies. Mason replied asking to schedule a call to discuss pricing details.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the per-seat pricing if we commit to expanding to the full team within 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Let's discuss on our pricing call - I can explore options with my leadership.

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation call with Mason. He confirmed Chorus quoted higher but is offering more brand recognition. Discussed options for volume discounts and contract terms. Mason requested a small discount to help justify choosing us over Chorus. Agreed to hold pricing at $2,900 with consulting included. Mason mentioned he wants to bring this to his VP before final signature.

questions_asked.question: If we sign annual, is there room to move on price?
questions_asked.answer: The $2,900 is already our best pricing for your team size with the consulting package included.

questions_asked.question: When would you want to get your VP involved in the conversation?
questions_asked.answer: I'll brief her first, then maybe we can do a quick call next week to address any final questions she has.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer decision for you?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, the product fits our needs well. I just need to make sure my VP is comfortable moving forward without the Chorus brand name.

objections: My VP might push back on not choosing the market leader, especially if the price difference isn't massive.
next_steps: Schedule exec meeting with VP of Sales

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment call with Mason and brief appearance from VP of Sales. Mason presented the business case and walked through the platform benefits. VP joined for 8 minutes, asked about security/compliance and customer support model, but didn't dive deep into product evaluation. Meeting felt somewhat rushed and VP deferred final decision to Mason. No clear next steps or timeline established for contract signature.

questions_asked.question: What does your customer support look like - do we get a dedicated CSM?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, all customers get a dedicated CSM with quarterly business reviews included in your package.

questions_asked.question: What else do you need from us to move forward?
questions_asked.answer: I think we're good - I'll talk to Mason about timing and we'll circle back.

next_steps: Mason to discuss internally and confirm next steps
